title: Housing Lottery Launches for 2171 Frederick Douglass Boulevard in Harlem, Manhattan
summary: The affordable housing lottery has launched for 2171 Frederick Douglass Boulevard, an eight-story mixed-use building in Harlem, Manhattan. Developed by B Plus Realty, LLC and designed by Raul Cabato, the structure yields 14 residences. Available on NYC Housing Connect are three units for residents at 130 percent of the area median income (AMI), ranging in eligible income from $124,286 to $227,500.
author: Vanessa Londono

title: Permits Filed for 64 Green Street in Greenpoint, Brooklyn
summary: Permits have been filed for a six-story residential building at 64 Green Street in Greenpoint, Brooklyn. Located between Franklin Street and West Street, the lot is closest to the Greenpoint Avenue subway station, served by the G train. Pete Jacov is listed as the owner behind the applications.
author: Vanessa Londono
